LLMMessage.name: LLMMessage
LLMMessage.purpose: Represents a message sent to or received from the LLM.
LLMMessage.behavior: Contains a role (user, assistant, or system) and content.

LLMResponse.name: LLMResponse
LLMResponse.purpose: Represents the response from the LLM.
LLMResponse.behavior: Contains content, tokens used, input tokens, and output tokens.

ILLMProvider.name: ILLMProvider
ILLMProvider.purpose: Defines the interface for an LLM provider.
ILLMProvider.behavior: Provides a complete method for generating text.

LLMConfig.name: LLMConfig
LLMConfig.purpose: Represents the configuration for an LLM provider.
LLMConfig.behavior: Contains provider, API key, and model.

LLMError.name: LLMError
LLMError.purpose: Represents an error that occurred while using the LLM.

RateLimitError.name: RateLimitError
RateLimitError.purpose: Represents a rate limit error that occurred while using the LLM.

Logger.name: Logger
Logger.purpose: Represents a logger that can output messages to the console.
Logger.behavior: Provides methods for logging at different levels (info, warn, error, debug).

logger.name: logger
logger.behavior: Provides a single instance of the Logger class.

RateLimiter.name: RateLimiter
RateLimiter.purpose: Represents a rate limiter that tracks timestamps of recent requests.
RateLimiter.behavior: Provides an acquire method that blocks until it is safe to proceed.

RateLimiter.name: RateLimiter
RateLimiter.behavior: Limits the number of requests within a time window.
RateLimiter.limitations: Does not handle edge cases correctly.

sleep.name: sleep
sleep.purpose: Provides a function that sleeps for a specified amount of time.
sleep.behavior: Returns a promise that resolves after the specified time.

CacheEntry.name: CacheEntry
CacheEntry.purpose: Represents a cache entry that contains a file context and its content hash.

cacheFileContext.name: cacheFileContext
cacheFileContext.purpose: Caches a file context in the shared cache.
cacheFileContext.behavior: Stores the file context in the cache with its content hash as the key.

getCachedFileContext.name: getCachedFileContext
getCachedFileContext.purpose: Retrieves a cached file context from the shared cache.
getCachedFileContext.behavior: Returns the cached file context if it exists, otherwise returns null.

hasCachedContext.name: hasCachedContext
hasCachedContext.purpose: Checks if a cached context exists for a given content hash.
hasCachedContext.behavior: Returns true if the cached context exists, otherwise returns false.

loadBranchState.name: loadBranchState
loadBranchState.purpose: Loads the branch state for a specific branch.
loadBranchState.behavior: Returns the branch state if it exists, otherwise returns null.

saveBranchState.name: saveBranchState
saveBranchState.purpose: Saves the branch state for the active branch.
saveBranchState.behavior: Stores the branch state in the branch-state.json file.

updateBranchState.name: updateBranchState
updateBranchState.purpose: Updates the branch state after a workflow run.
updateBranchState.behavior: Merges new file hashes and updates the headCommit.

removeFromBranchState.name: removeFromBranchState
removeFromBranchState.purpose: Removes deleted files from the branch state.
removeFromBranchState.behavior: Deletes the file hashes of the deleted files.

hasBranchIndex.name: hasBranchIndex
hasBranchIndex.purpose: Checks if a branch has been previously indexed.
hasBranchIndex.behavior: Returns true if the branch has been indexed, otherwise returns false.

batchCacheContexts.name: batchCacheContexts
batchCacheContexts.purpose: Batch-caches multiple file contexts into the shared cache and updates the branch state.
batchCacheContexts.behavior: Stores the file contexts in the cache and updates the branch state.

partitionByCacheHit.name: partitionByCacheHit
partitionByCacheHit.purpose: Partitions a list of files into cached and uncached files.
partitionByCacheHit.behavior: Returns an object with cached and uncached files.

OpenAIProvider.name: OpenAIProvider
OpenAIProvider.behavior: Provides a complete method for generating text.

GeminiProvider.name: GeminiProvider
GeminiProvider.behavior: Provides a complete method for generating text.

parseRetryDelay.name: parseRetryDelay
parseRetryDelay.purpose: Parses the retry delay from a 429 response.
parseRetryDelay.behavior: Returns the retry delay in milliseconds.

complete.name: complete
complete.purpose: Generate text based on user input.
complete.behavior: Makes a POST request to the Anthropic API with the user input and returns the generated text.
complete.limitations: Requires an API key and may be rate-limited.

complete.name: complete
complete.purpose: Generate text based on user input.
complete.behavior: Makes a POST request to the Ollama API with the user input and returns the generated text.
complete.limitations: Requires a locally-running Ollama instance and may be rate-limited.

complete.name: complete
complete.purpose: Generate text based on user input.
complete.behavior: Makes a POST request to the OpenAI-compatible server with the user input and returns the generated text.
complete.limitations: Requires an API key and may be rate-limited.

complete.name: complete
complete.purpose: Completes text based on input messages
complete.behavior: Makes a POST request to the server, handles errors and cancellation, returns LLMResponse
complete.limitations: May throw LLMError or RateLimitError, requires OpenAI-compatible local inference server

createLLMProvider.name: createLLMProvider
createLLMProvider.purpose: Create a language model provider instance.
createLLMProvider.behavior: Creates an instance of a language model provider based on the user's configuration and returns it.
createLLMProvider.limitations: Requires user configuration and may throw errors if configuration is invalid.

CodeAtlasStatusBar.name: CodeAtlasStatusBar
CodeAtlasStatusBar.purpose: Display the CodeAtlas status bar item.
CodeAtlasStatusBar.behavior: Creates a status bar item and updates its text and tooltip based on the CodeAtlas workflow status.
CodeAtlasStatusBar.limitations: Requires VS Code and may throw errors if VS Code is not available.

CodeAtlasActivityProvider.name: CodeAtlasActivityProvider
CodeAtlasActivityProvider.purpose: Display the CodeAtlas activity bar item.
CodeAtlasActivityProvider.behavior: Creates an activity bar item and updates its children based on the CodeAtlas workflow status.
CodeAtlasActivityProvider.limitations: Requires VS Code and may throw errors if VS Code is not available.

main.name: main
main.behavior: Resolves extension development path and tests path, then runs tests using test-electron.
main.limitations: Assumes test-electron is installed and configured correctly.

sanitizeBranchName.name: sanitizeBranchName
sanitizeBranchName.purpose: Sanitizes a branch name by replacing forward slashes with double dashes.
sanitizeBranchName.behavior: Replaces forward slashes with double dashes, leaving branch names without slashes unchanged.
sanitizeBranchName.limitations: Does not handle branch names with multiple consecutive slashes.

sanitizeBranchName.name: sanitizeBranchName
sanitizeBranchName.purpose: Sanitize a git branch name for use as a filesystem directory name
sanitizeBranchName.behavior: Replaces '/' with '--' in the branch name

chunkText.name: chunkText
chunkText.purpose: Chunks text into smaller pieces based on a maximum character limit.
chunkText.behavior: Splits text into chunks of maximum length, preserving text content across chunks.
chunkText.limitations: Does not handle text with non-ASCII characters correctly.

detectLanguage.name: detectLanguage
detectLanguage.purpose: Detects the language of a file based on its extension.
detectLanguage.behavior: Returns the detected language based on file extension, or 'Unknown' if unknown.
detectLanguage.limitations: Does not handle file extensions that are not in the language mapping.

detectLanguage.name: detectLanguage
detectLanguage.purpose: Detect the language of a file

getCodeAtlasDirForRoot.name: getCodeAtlasDirForRoot
getCodeAtlasDirForRoot.purpose: Returns the path to the .codeatlas directory for a given root directory.
getCodeAtlasDirForRoot.behavior: Returns the path to the .codeatlas directory, preserving drive letter on Windows.
getCodeAtlasDirForRoot.limitations: Does not handle root directories that are not strings.

getCodeAtlasDirForRoot.name: getCodeAtlasDirForRoot
getCodeAtlasDirForRoot.purpose: Get the CodeAtlas directory path for a given workspace root
getCodeAtlasDirForRoot.behavior: Returns the absolute path to the CodeAtlas directory

run.name: run
run.behavior: Creates a Mocha instance, adds test files, and runs tests.
run.limitations: Assumes test files are in the correct format and location.

acquire.name: acquire
acquire.purpose: Acquires a slot in the rate limiter.
acquire.behavior: Returns a promise that resolves when a slot is acquired.
acquire.limitations: Does not handle cases where the rate limiter is full.

usedInWindow.name: usedInWindow
usedInWindow.purpose: Returns the number of slots used in the time window.
usedInWindow.behavior: Returns the number of slots used in the time window.
usedInWindow.limitations: Does not handle cases where the time window is not set.

CODEATLAS_DIR.name: CODEATLAS_DIR
CODEATLAS_DIR.behavior: Returns the absolute path to the CodeAtlas directory

getWorkspaceRoot.name: getWorkspaceRoot
getWorkspaceRoot.behavior: Returns the absolute path to the workspace root directory
getWorkspaceRoot.limitations: Throws an error if no workspace folder is open

getCodeAtlasDir.name: getCodeAtlasDir
getCodeAtlasDir.behavior: Returns the absolute path to the CodeAtlas directory

getBranchDir.name: getBranchDir
getBranchDir.behavior: Returns the absolute path to the branch directory

getSharedCacheDir.name: getSharedCacheDir
getSharedCacheDir.purpose: Get the shared content-hash cache directory (`.codeatlas/cache/`)
getSharedCacheDir.behavior: Returns the absolute path to the cache directory

setActiveBranch.name: setActiveBranch
setActiveBranch.behavior: Sets the active branch for branch-aware path helpers

getActiveBranch.name: getActiveBranch
getActiveBranch.purpose: Get the currently active branch name
getActiveBranch.limitations: Throws an error if no active branch is set

getActiveBranchDir.name: getActiveBranchDir
getActiveBranchDir.purpose: Get the directory for the currently active branch
getActiveBranchDir.behavior: Returns the absolute path to the active branch directory

getContextDir.name: getContextDir
getContextDir.behavior: Returns the absolute path to the context directory

getGraphFilePath.name: getGraphFilePath
getGraphFilePath.behavior: Returns the absolute path to the graph file

getIndexFilePath.name: getIndexFilePath
getIndexFilePath.behavior: Returns the absolute path to the index file

getContextOverviewPath.name: getContextOverviewPath
getContextOverviewPath.purpose: Get the context overview file path
getContextOverviewPath.behavior: Returns the absolute path to the context overview file

getBranchStatePath.name: getBranchStatePath
getBranchStatePath.purpose: Get the branch state file path
getBranchStatePath.behavior: Returns the absolute path to the branch state file

getContextFilePath.name: getContextFilePath
getContextFilePath.behavior: Returns the absolute path to the context file

getCurrentIndexDir.name: getCurrentIndexDir
getCurrentIndexDir.behavior: Returns the absolute path to the current index directory

publishCurrentIndex.name: publishCurrentIndex
publishCurrentIndex.purpose: Copy the active branch's output files to `.codeatlas/current/`
publishCurrentIndex.behavior: Copies the index file, context overview file, graph file, and context directory

copyDirRecursive.name: copyDirRecursive
copyDirRecursive.behavior: Copies the directory and its contents

ensureDir.name: ensureDir
ensureDir.behavior: Creates the directory if it does not exist

ensureCodeAtlasDirs.name: ensureCodeAtlasDirs
ensureCodeAtlasDirs.purpose: Ensure the CodeAtlas directory structure exists
ensureCodeAtlasDirs.behavior: Creates the CodeAtlas directory and its subdirectories

writeJson.name: writeJson
writeJson.purpose: Write JSON data to a file
writeJson.behavior: Writes the JSON data to the file

readJson.name: readJson
readJson.purpose: Read JSON data from a file

writeText.name: writeText
writeText.behavior: Writes the text to the file

fileExists.name: fileExists
fileExists.behavior: Returns true if the file exists

getFileSizeKB.name: getFileSizeKB
getFileSizeKB.purpose: Get the file size in KB
getFileSizeKB.behavior: Returns the file size in KB

toRelativePath.name: toRelativePath
toRelativePath.purpose: Get the relative path from the workspace root

toAbsolutePath.name: toAbsolutePath
toAbsolutePath.purpose: Get the absolute path from a relative path

INCLUDE_FILE.name: INCLUDE_FILE
INCLUDE_FILE.behavior: Returns the absolute path to the .include.codeatlas file

isGitRepo.name: isGitRepo
isGitRepo.purpose: Check if the workspace root is inside a git repository
isGitRepo.behavior: Returns true if the workspace root is inside a git repository

getIncludeFilePath.name: getIncludeFilePath
getIncludeFilePath.purpose: Get the path to the .include.codeatlas file
getIncludeFilePath.behavior: Returns the absolute path to the .include.codeatlas file

hasIncludeFile.name: hasIncludeFile
hasIncludeFile.purpose: Check if a .include.codeatlas file exists in the workspace root
hasIncludeFile.behavior: Returns true if the .include.codeatlas file exists

parseIncludeFile.name: parseIncludeFile
parseIncludeFile.behavior: Returns the list of resolved entries

collectSourceFiles.name: collectSourceFiles
collectSourceFiles.purpose: Collect source files using the .include.codeatlas file
collectSourceFiles.behavior: Returns the list of source files

collectSourceFilesViaGlob.name: collectSourceFilesViaGlob
collectSourceFilesViaGlob.purpose: Collect files based on glob patterns

buildDependencyGraph.name: buildDependencyGraph
buildDependencyGraph.purpose: Builds a dependency graph of source files
buildDependencyGraph.behavior: Takes an array of source file paths, extracts symbols and import relationships, and returns a dependency graph object
buildDependencyGraph.limitations: Requires ctags to be installed and configured properly

isCtagsAvailable.name: isCtagsAvailable
isCtagsAvailable.purpose: Checks if ctags is available on the system
isCtagsAvailable.behavior: Runs a ctags command with the --version flag and checks for errors
isCtagsAvailable.limitations: May return false positives if ctags is not properly installed or configured

extractSymbolsWithCtags.name: extractSymbolsWithCtags
extractSymbolsWithCtags.purpose: Extracts symbols from source files using ctags
extractSymbolsWithCtags.behavior: Runs ctags with the --output-format=json flag and parses the output to extract symbols
extractSymbolsWithCtags.limitations: Requires ctags to be installed and configured properly

extractSymbolsWithRegex.name: extractSymbolsWithRegex
extractSymbolsWithRegex.purpose: Extracts symbols from source files using regex-based parsing
extractSymbolsWithRegex.behavior: Parses source files to extract symbols based on language-specific patterns
extractSymbolsWithRegex.limitations: May not cover all possible symbol types or languages

extractImports.name: extractImports
extractImports.purpose: Extracts import relationships from source files
extractImports.behavior: Parses source files to extract import statements based on language-specific patterns
extractImports.limitations: May not cover all possible import types or languages

extractImports.name: extractImports
extractImports.purpose: Extracts imports from a given code snippet
extractImports.behavior: Takes a code snippet, a file path, and a workspace root as input, and returns an array of imports extracted from the code
extractImports.limitations: Only supports a limited set of programming languages

buildImportGraph.name: buildImportGraph
buildImportGraph.purpose: Builds the import graph of source files
buildImportGraph.behavior: Parses source files to extract import relationships and updates the import graph
buildImportGraph.limitations: May not cover all possible import types or languages

normalizeKind.name: normalizeKind
normalizeKind.purpose: Normalizes the kind of a symbol
normalizeKind.behavior: Maps ctags kind codes to human-readable symbol types
normalizeKind.limitations: May not cover all possible kind codes

getLanguageFromPath.name: getLanguageFromPath
getLanguageFromPath.purpose: Determines the language of a source file
getLanguageFromPath.behavior: Parses the file extension to determine the language
getLanguageFromPath.limitations: May not cover all possible file extensions

ProviderStats.name: ProviderStats
ProviderStats.purpose: Represents statistics for a single LLM provider, including input/output tokens, request counts, and error rates.
ProviderStats.behavior: Stores and updates provider-specific statistics, such as input/output tokens, request counts, and error rates.
ProviderStats.limitations: Does not account for concurrent updates from multiple workflow runs.

UsageStatsData.name: UsageStatsData
UsageStatsData.purpose: Represents the overall usage statistics, including global workflow runs and provider-specific statistics.
UsageStatsData.behavior: Stores and updates the overall usage statistics, including global workflow runs and provider-specific statistics.
UsageStatsData.limitations: Does not account for concurrent updates from multiple workflow runs.

StatsDisplay.name: StatsDisplay
StatsDisplay.purpose: Represents the displayable statistics for a single provider, including averages and totals.
StatsDisplay.behavior: Calculates and displays the statistics for a single provider, including averages and totals.
StatsDisplay.limitations: Does not account for concurrent updates from multiple workflow runs.

UsageTracker.name: UsageTracker
UsageTracker.purpose: A lightweight accumulator that tracks usage statistics for a single workflow run.
UsageTracker.behavior: Records and updates usage statistics for a single workflow run, including input/output tokens, request counts, and error rates.
UsageTracker.limitations: Does not account for concurrent updates from multiple workflow runs.

UsageTracker.name: UsageTracker
UsageTracker.purpose: Tracks usage statistics for language models, including input tokens, output tokens, unsplit tokens, request count, and total duration.
UsageTracker.behavior: Accumulates statistics across multiple calls to the recordCall method, which updates the tracker's state based on the provided response and duration.
UsageTracker.limitations: Does not handle responses with missing token information.

getStatsDisplay.name: getStatsDisplay
getStatsDisplay.purpose: Calculates and returns the displayable statistics for a single provider.
getStatsDisplay.behavior: Calculates and returns the displayable statistics for a single provider, including averages and totals.
getStatsDisplay.limitations: Does not account for concurrent updates from multiple workflow runs.

getStatsDisplay.name: getStatsDisplay
getStatsDisplay.purpose: Computes and formats usage statistics for display, including total tokens, average input tokens per run, average output tokens per run, and average files per run.
getStatsDisplay.behavior: Takes a UsageStatsData object as input and returns a StatsDisplay object, which contains the formatted statistics.
getStatsDisplay.limitations: Does not handle cases where workflow runs is 0.

loadUsageStats.name: loadUsageStats
loadUsageStats.purpose: Loads the persisted usage statistics from disk.
loadUsageStats.behavior: Loads the persisted usage statistics from disk and returns the data as a UsageStatsData object.
loadUsageStats.limitations: Does not account for concurrent updates from multiple workflow runs.

saveUsageStats.name: saveUsageStats
saveUsageStats.purpose: Saves the usage statistics to disk.
saveUsageStats.behavior: Saves the usage statistics to disk, overwriting any existing data.
saveUsageStats.limitations: Does not account for concurrent updates from multiple workflow runs.

persistWorkflowUsage.name: persistWorkflowUsage
persistWorkflowUsage.purpose: Merges a completed workflow run's tracker into the persisted stats.
persistWorkflowUsage.behavior: Merges a completed workflow run's tracker into the persisted stats, updating the provider-specific statistics and global workflow runs.
persistWorkflowUsage.limitations: Does not account for concurrent updates from multiple workflow runs.

resetUsageStats.name: resetUsageStats
resetUsageStats.behavior: Resets all persisted usage statistics, overwriting any existing data.
resetUsageStats.limitations: Does not account for concurrent updates from multiple workflow runs.

analyzeFile.name: analyzeFile
analyzeFile.purpose: Analyzes a single file using the LLM.
analyzeFile.behavior: Takes a file node, dependency graph, LLM provider, rate limiter, and optional parameters to analyze the file and return a FileContext object.
analyzeFile.limitations: May throw RateLimitError or AbortError if rate limiting is exceeded or the analysis is cancelled.

analyzeFileBatch.name: analyzeFileBatch
analyzeFileBatch.purpose: Analyzes a batch of files using the LLM.
analyzeFileBatch.behavior: Takes a list of file nodes, dependency graph, LLM provider, rate limiter, and optional parameters to analyze the files in batches and return a list of FileContext objects.
analyzeFileBatch.limitations: May throw RateLimitError or AbortError if rate limiting is exceeded or the analysis is cancelled.

buildDependencyContext.name: buildDependencyContext
buildDependencyContext.purpose: Builds a dependency context for a file node.
buildDependencyContext.behavior: Takes a file node and dependency graph to extract the dependencies of the file and return a string representation of the dependencies.
buildDependencyContext.limitations: May return an empty string if the file has no dependencies.

callWithRetry.name: callWithRetry
callWithRetry.purpose: Retries a function up to MAX_RETRIES times on RateLimitError or transient network errors.
callWithRetry.behavior: Takes a function to retry and a label to log the retry attempts.
callWithRetry.limitations: May throw RateLimitError or AbortError if rate limiting is exceeded or the analysis is cancelled.

extractJson.name: extractJson
extractJson.purpose: Robustly extracts a JSON object from an LLM response.
extractJson.behavior: Takes an LLM response to extract the JSON object and return it as a JavaScript object.
extractJson.limitations: May return null if the LLM response is not valid JSON.

normalizeSymbols.name: normalizeSymbols
normalizeSymbols.purpose: Normalizes a list of symbols from the LLM's output.
normalizeSymbols.behavior: Takes a list of symbols to normalize and return a list of SymbolContext objects.
normalizeSymbols.limitations: May return an empty list if the input list is empty.

buildIndex.name: buildIndex
buildIndex.purpose: Builds a semantic index from file contexts and dependency graphs.
buildIndex.behavior: Iterates over file contexts, extracts symbol information, and constructs the index.
buildIndex.limitations: Requires file contexts and dependency graphs as input.

buildIndex.name: buildIndex
buildIndex.purpose: Builds a semantic index of code files
buildIndex.behavior: Takes a list of file contexts and a dependency graph as input and returns a semantic index
buildIndex.limitations: Requires a valid dependency graph and file contexts

persistIndex.name: persistIndex
persistIndex.purpose: Persists the semantic index to a file.
persistIndex.behavior: Writes the index to a JSON file.
persistIndex.limitations: Requires a valid semantic index as input.

persistContextOverview.name: persistContextOverview
persistContextOverview.purpose: Generates a context overview file from the semantic index and dependency graph.
persistContextOverview.behavior: Constructs a markdown file with codebase statistics, usage instructions, and file overviews.
persistContextOverview.limitations: Requires a valid semantic index and dependency graph as input.

indexKeywords.name: indexKeywords
indexKeywords.purpose: Indexes keywords from symbol purposes and behaviors.
indexKeywords.behavior: Extracts meaningful words from text and stores them in a keyword index.
indexKeywords.limitations: Requires a symbol context, file path, and keyword index as input.

extractMeaningfulWords.name: extractMeaningfulWords
extractMeaningfulWords.behavior: Removes stop words, punctuation, and short words from text.

extractMeaningfulWords.name: extractMeaningfulWords
extractMeaningfulWords.purpose: Extracts meaningful words from a given text
extractMeaningfulWords.behavior: Takes a string as input and returns a list of meaningful words
extractMeaningfulWords.limitations: May not work correctly with non-English text or text with special characters

inlineOverview.name: inlineOverview
inlineOverview.purpose: Formats an overview string for inline use in a markdown list item.
inlineOverview.behavior: Collapses newlines, truncates text, and falls back to a placeholder if empty.
inlineOverview.limitations: Requires a string or undefined as input.

getCurrentBranch.name: getCurrentBranch
getCurrentBranch.purpose: Returns the current branch name, or HEAD commit hash if detached, or 'default' for empty repos.
getCurrentBranch.behavior: Tries to get the current branch using 'git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD', falling back to 'git rev-parse --short HEAD' if that fails, and returning 'default' if the repo is empty.

getHeadCommit.name: getHeadCommit
getHeadCommit.purpose: Returns the commit hash that HEAD points to.
getHeadCommit.behavior: Tries to get the commit hash using 'git rev-parse HEAD', returning an empty string if that fails.

getChangedFilesBetweenRefs.name: getChangedFilesBetweenRefs
getChangedFilesBetweenRefs.purpose: Get files that differ between two refs (branches/commits).
getChangedFilesBetweenRefs.behavior: Uses 'git diff --name-status' to get the files that were added, modified, or deleted between the two refs.

getUncommittedChanges.name: getUncommittedChanges
getUncommittedChanges.purpose: Get uncommitted changes (both staged and unstaged) relative to HEAD.
getUncommittedChanges.behavior: Uses 'git diff --name-status HEAD' and 'git diff --name-status --cached' to get the modified and deleted files, and 'git ls-files -o --exclude-standard' to get the untracked files.

getAllChangedFiles.name: getAllChangedFiles
getAllChangedFiles.purpose: Get ALL files that have changed relative to the last indexed state.
getAllChangedFiles.behavior: Combines branch diff + uncommitted changes into a single set.

getFileContentHash.name: getFileContentHash
getFileContentHash.purpose: Compute a content hash for a file using the same algorithm as git (SHA-1 of blob).
getFileContentHash.behavior: Uses 'git hash-object' if in a git repo, falling back to SHA-256 of content.

getFileHashAtRef.name: getFileHashAtRef
getFileHashAtRef.purpose: Get the git blob hash for a file at a specific ref (commit/branch).
getFileHashAtRef.behavior: Uses 'git rev-parse' to get the hash of the file at the specified ref.

captureGitState.name: captureGitState
captureGitState.behavior: Returns an object with the current branch, head commit, and timestamp.

WorkflowOptions.name: WorkflowOptions
WorkflowOptions.purpose: Configuration options for the CodeAtlas workflow
WorkflowOptions.behavior: Defines the settings for the workflow, including rate limits, file size limits, and concurrency options
WorkflowOptions.limitations: Must be an object with the specified properties

WorkflowResult.name: WorkflowResult
WorkflowResult.purpose: Result object for the CodeAtlas workflow
WorkflowResult.behavior: Contains information about the workflow's progress and outcome
WorkflowResult.limitations: Must be an object with the specified properties

activateBranch.name: activateBranch
activateBranch.purpose: Activate the current branch before running the workflow
activateBranch.behavior: Resolves and sets the active branch, returning the branch name and current HEAD commit
activateBranch.limitations: Must be called before running the workflow

runFullWorkflow.name: runFullWorkflow
runFullWorkflow.behavior: Analyzes all source files, builds a dependency graph, and creates a semantic index
runFullWorkflow.limitations: Must be called with a valid LLM provider and workflow options

runIncrementalUpdate.name: runIncrementalUpdate
runIncrementalUpdate.purpose: Run an incremental update of the CodeAtlas workflow
runIncrementalUpdate.behavior: Updates the semantic index with changes to source files
runIncrementalUpdate.limitations: Must be called with a valid LLM provider and workflow options

runSingleFileAnalysis.name: runSingleFileAnalysis
runSingleFileAnalysis.purpose: Run a single-file analysis of the CodeAtlas workflow
runSingleFileAnalysis.behavior: Analyzes a single source file and creates a semantic index for it
runSingleFileAnalysis.limitations: Must be called with a valid file path, LLM provider, and workflow options

topologicalSort.name: topologicalSort
topologicalSort.purpose: Perform a topological sort on a dependency graph
topologicalSort.behavior: Returns a list of nodes in topological order
topologicalSort.limitations: Must be called with a valid dependency graph

checkCancellation.name: checkCancellation
checkCancellation.purpose: Check if a cancellation request has been made
checkCancellation.behavior: Throws a cancellation error if the token is cancelled
checkCancellation.limitations: Must be called with a valid cancellation token

runConcurrent.name: runConcurrent
runConcurrent.purpose: Run an array of async tasks with concurrency
runConcurrent.behavior: Returns a list of task outcomes
runConcurrent.limitations: Must be called with a valid array of tasks and concurrency level

LocalProvider.name: LocalProvider
LocalProvider.purpose: Connects to OpenAI-compatible local inference servers for text completion tasks
LocalProvider.behavior: Supports early termination and cancellation, handles different server types and error cases
LocalProvider.limitations: Requires OpenAI-compatible local inference server, may not work with all server types

readStream.name: readStream
readStream.purpose: Reads an SSE stream from the server
readStream.behavior: Tracks JSON brace depth, handles early termination and cancellation, returns LLMResponse
readStream.limitations: May throw DOMException or LLMError, requires SSE stream from server

readNonStreamingResponse.name: readNonStreamingResponse
readNonStreamingResponse.purpose: Reads a non-streaming response from the server
readNonStreamingResponse.behavior: Handles different response formats, returns LLMResponse
readNonStreamingResponse.limitations: May throw LLMError, requires non-streaming response from server

ServerManager.name: ServerManager
ServerManager.purpose: Singleton class for managing a llama-server child process.
ServerManager.behavior: Provides methods for starting, stopping, and monitoring the server, as well as emitting events to registered callbacks.
ServerManager.limitations: Only one instance of the server can run at a time.

start.name: start
start.purpose: Starts the llama-server child process with the given options.
start.behavior: Checks if the server is already running, stops it if necessary, and then starts the new server with the provided options.
start.limitations: Fails if the model file or llama-server binary is not found.

stop.name: stop
stop.behavior: Kills the server process and emits a 'stopped' event to registered callbacks.
stop.limitations: Fails if the server process is not found.

dispose.name: dispose
dispose.purpose: Disposes of the llama-server child process and clears any registered callbacks.
dispose.behavior: Kills the server process and clears any registered callbacks.

pollHealth.name: pollHealth
pollHealth.purpose: Polls the server's health endpoint to determine its status.
pollHealth.behavior: Sends a GET request to the server's health endpoint and updates the server's status accordingly.
pollHealth.limitations: Fails if the server is not running or the health endpoint is not available.

addLog.name: addLog
addLog.purpose: Adds a log line to the server's log buffer.
addLog.behavior: Pushes the log line to the server's log buffer and emits an event to registered callbacks.
addLog.limitations: Does not emit any events if the log buffer is full.

emit.name: emit
emit.purpose: Emits an event to registered callbacks.
emit.behavior: Updates the server's status and emits an event to registered callbacks.
emit.limitations: Does not emit any events if the server is not running.

detectBinary.name: detectBinary
detectBinary.purpose: Detects the llama-server binary on the system.
detectBinary.behavior: Checks a list of possible binary locations and returns the first one that exists.
detectBinary.limitations: Fails if none of the binary locations are found.

commandExists.name: commandExists
commandExists.purpose: Checks if a command exists on the system.
commandExists.behavior: Executes a command to check if the binary exists and returns a promise with the result.
commandExists.limitations: Fails if the command execution fails.

activate.name: activate
activate.purpose: Initializes the CodeAtlas extension and sets up its functionality
activate.behavior: Creates and configures the main panel, status bar, and activity bar, and registers commands for user interactions
activate.limitations: Must be called once when the extension is activated

deactivate.name: deactivate
deactivate.purpose: Cleans up and disposes of the CodeAtlas extension's resources
deactivate.behavior: Disposes of the auto-update timer, server manager, and logger
deactivate.limitations: Must be called when the extension is deactivated

setupAutoUpdate.name: setupAutoUpdate
setupAutoUpdate.purpose: Sets up the auto-update functionality for the CodeAtlas extension
setupAutoUpdate.behavior: Configures the file watcher and branch polling, and registers commands for user interactions
setupAutoUpdate.limitations: Must be called once when the extension is activated

disposeAutoUpdate.name: disposeAutoUpdate
disposeAutoUpdate.purpose: Cleans up and disposes of the auto-update resources
disposeAutoUpdate.behavior: Clears the branch polling interval and file watcher
disposeAutoUpdate.limitations: Must be called when the extension is deactivated

buildWorkflowOptions.name: buildWorkflowOptions
buildWorkflowOptions.purpose: Builds the workflow options for the CodeAtlas extension
buildWorkflowOptions.behavior: Configures the workflow options based on the user's configuration
buildWorkflowOptions.limitations: Must be called when running a workflow

handleWorkflowError.name: handleWorkflowError
handleWorkflowError.purpose: Handles errors that occur during workflow execution
handleWorkflowError.behavior: Displays an error message to the user and logs the error
handleWorkflowError.limitations: Must be called when an error occurs during workflow execution

extractSymbolsFromContent.name: extractSymbolsFromContent
extractSymbolsFromContent.purpose: Extracts symbols from a given code snippet
extractSymbolsFromContent.behavior: Takes a code snippet and a file path as input, and returns an array of symbols extracted from the code
extractSymbolsFromContent.limitations: Only supports a limited set of programming languages

recordCall.name: recordCall
recordCall.purpose: Updates the UsageTracker's state based on the provided response and duration.
recordCall.behavior: Accumulates statistics across multiple calls, including input tokens, output tokens, unsplit tokens, request count, and total duration.
recordCall.limitations: Does not handle responses with missing token information.

makeStats.name: makeStats
makeStats.purpose: Creates a UsageStatsData object with default values and optional overrides.
makeStats.behavior: Returns a UsageStatsData object with the specified provider name and overrides.

src/llm/types.ts.overview: Defines types and interfaces for the LLM (Large Language Model) module.

src/utils/logger.ts.overview: Provides a logging utility for the application.

src/llm/rateLimiter.ts.overview: Provides a rate limiter for the LLM module.

src/core/cache/contextCache.ts.overview: Provides a cache for file contexts.

src/llm/providers/claude.ts.overview: ClaudeProvider connects to the Anthropic API to generate text based on user input.

src/llm/providers/ollama.ts.overview: OllamaProvider connects to a locally-running Ollama instance using its native NDJSON streaming API.

src/llm/providers/openaiCompat.ts.overview: OpenAICompatProvider connects to a remote server that exposes the OpenAI /v1/chat/completions API.

src/llm/factory.ts.overview: LLM factory function that creates an instance of a language model provider based on the user's configuration.

src/vscode/statusBar.ts.overview: CodeAtlasStatusBar class that displays the CodeAtlas status bar item in the VS Code status bar.

src/vscode/activityBar.ts.overview: CodeAtlasActivityProvider class that displays the CodeAtlas activity bar item in the VS Code activity bar.

src/test/index.ts.overview: Runs tests for the extension using the test-electron module.

src/test/suite/index.ts.overview: Runs tests for the extension using Mocha.

src/utils/fileUtils.ts.overview: This file provides utility functions for file system operations, branch management, and workspace configuration. It exports constants, functions, and variables for working with the CodeAtlas directory structure, branch tracking, and file path manipulation.

src/core/analyzer/graphBuilder.ts.overview: This file is responsible for building a dependency graph of source files, including their symbols and import relationships. It uses a combination of ctags and regex-based parsing to extract symbols and import statements.

src/core/stats/usageStats.ts.overview: This file provides functionality for tracking and persisting usage statistics of language models (LLMs) across multiple workflow runs, including input/output tokens, request counts, and error rates.

src/core/analyzer/fileAnalyzer.ts.overview: This file contains the main logic for analyzing a single file or a batch of files using the LLM (Large Language Model). It provides functions for analyzing files individually or in batches, handling rate limiting, and parsing the LLM's output to extract relevant information.

src/core/analyzer/indexer.ts.overview: This file, `indexer.ts`, is responsible for building a semantic index of a codebase, which includes a flat symbol index, a file index, and an inverted keyword index. It aggregates data from individual file analyses and dependency graphs to provide a comprehensive understanding of the codebase.

src/core/git/gitService.ts.overview: This file provides a set of Git-related utility functions for managing branches, diffs, and content hashing. It allows for retrieving branch information, computing content hashes, and capturing the current Git state.

src/core/workflow/runner.ts.overview: This file contains the core logic for the CodeAtlas workflow, which analyzes source files and builds a semantic index for code understanding and navigation.

src/llm/providers/local.ts.overview: LocalProvider is a class that connects to OpenAI-compatible local inference servers for text completion tasks, supporting early termination and cancellation.

src/server/serverManager.ts.overview: Manages a llama-server child process, providing a singleton instance for starting, stopping, and monitoring the server.

src/extension.ts.overview: This file is the main entry point for the CodeAtlas extension, responsible for initializing and managing the extension's functionality, including setting up the UI, handling user interactions, and performing analysis tasks.

src/test/suite/graphBuilder.test.ts.overview: This file contains unit tests for the GraphBuilder class, specifically for the extractSymbolsFromContent and extractImports methods. The tests cover various programming languages, including TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Go, Rust, and Java, to ensure the GraphBuilder can correctly extract symbols and imports from code in different languages.

src/test/suite/indexer.test.ts.overview: This file contains unit tests for the indexer functionality, which is responsible for building a semantic index of code files. The tests cover various aspects of the indexer, including building the index structure, counting symbols, handling symbol collisions, and populating the keyword index.

src/test/suite/usageStats.test.ts.overview: This file contains unit tests for the UsageTracker class and the getStatsDisplay function, which are used to track and display usage statistics for language models.
